title: Play Kitchen Stove and Sink with Running Water
summary: A matching pair of toy kitchen base cabinets — a sink unit built around a purchased running-water toy sink, and a stove unit with an acrylic "glass" cooktop — each with a hinged cabinet door below.
description:
This is genuinely two builds sharing one construction method — a sink base and a stove base, sized around their specific inserts. Measure your own sink and adjust the plan as needed before cutting anything; the cut list is built around a sink roughly 16" x 10-3/4", and a different model will shift several dimensions.

Build both bases the same way: sides to toekick, front to sides, bottom shelf, back top support and interior shelf, then the back panel — nailed and glued at every joint, no screws needed in the case itself. The doors are the one place accuracy matters most: build each door frame to the actual opening (not a fixed number), keep the plywood door-back panel about 1" larger than the frame's center opening, and hang with surface-mount hinges and a magnetic clasp so a toddler can actually open it.

materials.name: Pine, 1x3, 8 ft
materials.note: Toekicks, back top supports, and door frames for both units.

materials.name: 1/4" plywood, 2 ft x 2 ft plus scrap for door backs
Toy sink with running water, about 16" x 10-3/4"

cutList.part: Sink toekick and back top support

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood, 2 ft x 2 ft plus scrap for door backs

cutList.part: Stove toekick and back top support

cutList.material: 1/4" plywood, 2 ft x 2 ft plus scrap for door backs

steps.title: Attach the sides to the toekick
steps.body: For each unit, attach the sides to the toekick with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the front
steps.body: Attach the front to the sides, flush on top and sides, with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Attach the bottom shelf
steps.body: Attach the bottom shelf to the sides and the toekick with 1-1/4" brad nails and wood glue.

steps.title: Add the back top support and interior shelf
steps.body: Attach the back top support to the back top corner with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue. Attach the interior 1x10 shelf, flush to the back, with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue, leaving about 6" of space under the bottom shelf.

steps.title: Attach the back panel
steps.body: Attach the plywood back panel with 5/8" brad nails and wood glue.
materials: 1/4" plywood, 2 ft x 2 ft plus scrap for door backs

steps.title: Build and attach the tops
steps.body: For the sink unit, attach the top frame with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Then attach the top pieces to the tops of both the stove and sink units with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and hang the doors
steps.body: Build each door frame from 1x3s sized to the actual opening, joined with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws (two per joint). Attach a 1/4" plywood panel to the back of each frame with 5/8" brad nails and glue — the panel should be about 1" larger than the frame's center opening, not the full door size. On the stove, attach the acrylic glass cooktop with a suitable glue or predrilled small hardware screws. Hang each door with surface mount hinges on the side and a magnetic clasp to keep it closed, then add knobs or handles.
materials: 1/4" plywood, 2 ft x 2 ft plus scrap for door backs

images.alt: A matching toy kitchen sink and stove base cabinet, each with a hinged door
